derivation: Panel: worldgen 40, player 40, rendering 40, systems 42, perf 40. Median = 40 and four of five judges land exactly there independently, which is unusually tight. The systems 42 is the outlier and gets discounted rather than averaged in: it carries one demonstrable overcredit (it passed the held-item viewmodel wow off the probe screenshot, while both the player and rendering judges refuted it with a code-grounded scene-graph audit showing no camera-attached mesh exists anywhere in the file) and the loosest pricing of the light family (2/3 weights for a distance-field model with no occlusion). Stripping those puts systems at ~40-40.5, i.e. consensus. No upward adjustment is warranted either: the round added a new verified fail (vertically-escaped mobs leak forever through the horizontal-only despawn check at index.html:806-807), the pointer-lock/collision cluster is dark a fifth consecutive round (~12 checklist points withheld), and weather/smelting/redstone remain confirmed-absent families. Merged round_mps = 40.0, a +2.5 delta over round 4's 37.5. The delta is real and conversion-driven, not advance credit: cave darkness + torch light verified numerically (lightAt 0.1 -> 0.876) and visually with save/load persistence, the complete farming loop with exact hunger math, the knockback fail fixed and measured to the decimal (1.02-block shove), natural spawn ecology + despawn + sun-burn + light-gated spawning all live via the new deterministic step/tickSpawns/spawnAt hooks, swim-up converted after four rounds, audio converted via OfflineAudioContext peaks, and seed-1337 determinism (spawn 116.5/29.02/115.5, findStructures registry) bit-identical through all three lanes.

probe.observations: ENVIRONMENT: the new lane-1 hooks (step/tickSpawns/spawnAt) completely fixed last round's frame-pump starvation — I ran ~40s of deterministic sim across the session with zero drift and zero errors (__vox.errors empty and console clean except the pre-existing three.js r160 deprecation warning, across two loads). Sim cost measured 0.013 ms/frame with 8 zombies active, so the render budget is untouched; HUD FPS still reads 0 headlessly (same sampling artifact as round 4 — do not dock perf on my account). HUD coordinate line is still throttled/one-frame stale in screenshots. GAME FEEL: the cave-light system transforms the game — the dark-cave vs torch-lit pair is genuinely dramatic (pitch-black stone barely readable at the 0.1 floor, then warm fully-readable interior with coal flecks visible and a held-torch viewmodel), and torch light gates zombie spawns for real gameplay consequence. Farming is a complete, legible loop with three genuinely distinct crop visuals and a charming furrowed farmland texture. The knockback fix reads great (1.0-1.4 block shove with decaying impulse + vertical pop). GLITCHES/QUIRKS for judges: (1) spawnMob with wrong arity spawns a mob at NaN/midair coords that falls through bedrock forever WITHOUT being despawned (distance-despawn appears horizontal-only; vertical runaway mobs leak) — harness-only path, but a robustness wart. (2) The night-time SURFACE torch light pool is subtle in screenshots (numeric torch component 0.49-0.76 is correct; underground contrast is where it shines). (3) My stone-mine at hardness ~2.8s did not complete after 2.9s of stepped sim (crack appeared, then released) — likely the mineHold real/synthetic-time seam, not re-verified since round-3 timed it; minor. (4) One pig of 4 stood still over 3s of wander (others moved 1-4 blocks) — normal idle. (5) Breaking the support under a torch still leaves it floating (known scope hold). UNVERIFIED THIS ROUND (don't award/dock on my account): biome-coherent border walk, rivers, overhangs, iron-at-depth dig, hold-to-break full rhythm, sneak edge-guard (sneak flag + lower eye verified, edge guard untested). Screenshot inventory: dark cave, torch-lit cave (x2 angles), 3 farm stages, sun-burn green particle burst, pig trio, night torch, hotbar with 9 textured item icons, crack overlay mid-mine, dirt-particle hole, underwater with bubble meter, snow-capped summit (day + night). The world now has light, agriculture, and consequences — easily the most alive round yet.

top_gaps.title: Torch-light occlusion / flood-fill upgrade (the family's last weight)
top_gaps.detail: lightOf (index.html:423) is radial distance only: torch light shines through solid walls, and heightmap skylight means player roofs never darken and surface digs never brighten the cave below — a sharp-eyed probe screenshot of light bleeding through a wall would read as a fail next round. A small per-chunk BFS through air cells (seeded from torches + sky columns) folded into the existing hcol/tlist precompute closes the weight-3 family and fixes the correctness hole. Touches the mesher hot path that just survived one high-risk round — isolate it and re-run the seam/AO/border regression suite.

top_gaps.title: Overhang/cliff carving so the terrain stretch stops being heightmap-only
top_gaps.detail: Five rounds, no overhang: the generator is strictly heightmap + interior carve, and the h-y>=3 gate suppresses surface breaches. Add a slope-gated 3D density bite (only where neighboring rawH differs by >=4, carve the upper face cells) so cliffs undercut into walkable ledges; keep it a pure function of (x,y,z,seed) so chunk borders and the seed-1337 spawn stay deterministic — verify spawn and findStructures bit-identical before anything else. Converts the stretch and feeds the cave-mouth wow.

top_gaps.title: Make one river findable
top_gaps.detail: Fifth round of dead code: either add a __vox helper scanning the rv field for the nearest channel crossing within 500 of spawn and have the probe fly it, or widen sst(.06,.02) until a channel demonstrably exists near spawn. Converts the wow, validates the header's rivers-feed-seas claim, and costs almost nothing; if the field genuinely produces no channel in the spawn region, delete the header claim — advertised-absent features invite open-dimension docks.

top_gaps.title: Vertical entity containment guard (the round's only verified fail)
top_gaps.detail: rmMob anything with !isFinite(M.x+M.y+M.z) or M.y<-16 in updMobs' existing per-mob loop (the pd>56 check at index.html:807 is horizontal-only). Two lines, clears the probe's filed fail, and hardens spawnMob against future harness/edge-case coordinate poisoning before villages/cave-spawning multiply entity entry points.

panel.summary: Round 5 is the biggest worldgen-felt delta of the run: the world finally has light as a property of terrain — a natural 41-cell cavern measured at the 0.1 skylight floor renders near-black and a placed torch verifiably brightens it to 0.876 with falloff and save/load persistence — while the snow line and mountain-shape stretch items paid their two-round verification debt with the h=50 summit screenshots. Natural spawn ecology converted on worldgen's own terms (night zombies and day pigs on validated grass columns, despawn live, torch light gating spawns via computed light rather than a time proxy), and farming grafted onto worldgen-scattered tufts without touching determinism: seed-1337 spawn and the structure registry stayed bit-identical through all three lanes. What still caps my domain: rivers and overhangs are five-round no-shows, coherent regions and foliage tint remain fixed-but-unwitnessed, structures sit at a third of their weight, the light engine is falloff math rather than propagation (no occlusion, roofs never darken), and one verified fail landed at the world's entity floor (vertically-escaped mobs leak through bedrock). I move 37 to 40 against round 4's merged 37.5: the 50-band is now fully play-verified end to end and two named 65-band ingredients (farming, caves-that-matter) are real, with ties broken downward for the unconverted residue and the absent weather/smelting/redstone families.

top_gaps.title: Mob containment fix: vertical despawn + NaN spawn guard
top_gaps.detail: The round's one verified fail: spawnMob accepts unvalidated coords and the despawn check (index.html:806-807) is horizontal-only, so a NaN/escaped mob falls forever below bedrock without cleanup. top_gaps.title: Close the mineHold synthetic-time seam
top_gaps.detail: The probe's stone-mine did not complete after 2.9s of stepped sim (crack appeared, then released): mineTick's mtLast clock mixes real performance.now() frames with step()'s synthetic t0+t*1000 base (index.html:666, 1134-1135), so real rAF frames interleaving between evals can reset or stall accumulation. Drive mineTick from the same clock simStep receives, or have step() snapshot/restore mtLast. Protects the round-3 timed-mining evidence from regression doubt and makes hold-to-break verifiable under deterministic sim.

top_gaps.title: Smelting + furnace (the last cheap full-family flip)

A furnace block (craftable from stone, placeable, right-click UI reusing the crafting panel) smelting iron ore -> ingot and porkchop -> cooked porkchop, with the iron-pick recipe migrated to ingots, closes the rubric's explicitly-named band-65 ingredient and retires the acknowledged ore-for-ingot substitution. Mostly additive: new item ids, one UI mode, a fuel/time ticker like updFarm.

top_gaps.title: Weather: rain with sky dimming and biome-aware snow
top_gaps.detail: Weight-2 family, zero code today. A seeded rain cycle (particle sheet around the camera reusing the existing particle buffer discipline, darker skyC blend, optional rain audio layer via the existing noise bus) plus snowflakes in snowy biomes converts a whole family with no worldgen or save-format risk. Watch the PM=320 particle cap and pause-freeze coupling.

top_gaps.title: Villages: one multi-building hamlet template (remaining ~2 of 3 weights)
top_gaps.detail: Micro-ruins banked ~1 weight; the family wants settlements. A 2-3 hut cluster (plank walls, log corners, torch inside each, a farmland plot) seeded like the 1-in-37 micro-structures but on a rarer grid with a flatness scan would convert the rest. This is the lane where the floating-islet class of regression lives — cross-chunk tset writes and determinism need the round-4 structure tests rerun.

top_gaps.title: Redstone-lite: lever + wire + lamp/door loop (partial ~2 of 4 weights)
top_gaps.detail: The biggest untouched weight. Even a minimal powered set (lever block toggling, wire that conducts along adjacent cells, a lamp that feeds the existing torch-light registry, a 2-block door) earns half the family. High risk: it introduces block state + update propagation into a stateless setB world and touches the mesher; do not attempt in the same round as villages.

top_gaps.title: Combat depth: sword, armor, and 1-2 new mobs
top_gaps.detail: Hostile/passive families are now maxed, but band-65 'combat with stakes' and the open dimension still pay for a craftable sword (replacing the unverified pick-bonus path with a real weapon tier), 2-4 armor points reducing zombie damage, and a skeleton (ranged pressure) or sheep/cow (wool/leather economy). Reuses the proven entity substrate, so scope is content more than systems.

top_gaps.title: Light occlusion pass (remaining ~1 light weight)
top_gaps.detail: The current engine is a distance field: torch light crosses solid walls and skylight is column-depth only, so a torch behind a 1-block wall lights your face — visible wrongness Minecraft never shows. A per-chunk BFS flood fill (or even a single occlusion raycast per torch-face pair) plus popping torches when support breaks would finish the family. Risk is real: lightOf is the hottest path in buildChunk and cross-border remesh correctness is fragile.

top_gaps.title: Robustness: vertical despawn + mineHold time seam
top_gaps.detail: Two probe-found warts: add a y<-32 (or NaN-coords) cull to the mob despawn check at line 806 so vertically-escaped entities can't leak forever, and route mineTick off the synthetic step() clock so stepped-sim mining completes deterministically. Twenty minutes of work that removes this round's only verified fail and a recurring probe trap before either bites a future round.

panel.summary: Round 5 is the round my domain stopped owing the panel evidence: everything round 4's systems judge advance-credited got play-confirmed (natural spawn ticks, despawn, sun-burn, and the knockback fail fixed with a measured 1.02-block shove), and two genuinely new families landed real — farming is a complete verified loop from position-hashed tuft seeds through three distinct growth stages to bread eating for exactly +5, and the cave-light/torch system is gameplay-coupled (0.1-floor dark caves, 0.876 torch pools, light-gated zombie spawns, save-persistent registry), not a shader trick. By family math I now credit ~29 of 40 systems weights versus ~22 last round, which puts the band-50 survival loop fully satisfied and verified for the first time and three band-65 ingredients (farming, caves that matter, combat) on the board; I land at 42, a +4.5 delta over the 37.5 merge and +2 over my predecessor's partially-discounted 40, held down by the light engine's no-occlusion shortcut, the floating-torch and OOB-mob warts, and the four still-absent families. The cheapest MPS next round is the smelting+weather pair (two low-risk full-family flips worth ~1.8 combined), then a village template; redstone is the largest remaining weight but should run alone given its blast radius.

panel.confidence: High on stability and sim cost — this is the best-instrumented round of the run: ~40s of deterministic sim with zero drift, __vox.errors empty across two loads, and a direct measurement of 0.013 ms/frame sim cost with 8 zombies active. Medium on rendering throughput: live FPS was again unmeasurable under the visibility-throttled harness (main-loop evidence asserts 120 when actually rendering; I accept it per the exoneration note), and remesh hitch cost (the 4-chunk remeshTorch burst, farming growth remeshes) was never directly timed. Residual swing ~±1: torch-dense worlds are unprofiled (per-face x per-torch sqrt in buildChunk), and the carried memory-growth gaps bite only on sessions longer than any probe has run.

item_verdicts.evidence: All three new Maps are bounded by explicit player actions (torch place/break maintains torchR symmetrically in setB; farm self-cleans; editB mirrors the save diff); no unbounded path added this round.

top_gaps.title: Vertical + finite-coordinate mob containment guard
top_gaps.detail: The round's only verified fail: despawn is horizontal-only (index.html:806), so a mob with bad/NaN coords falls forever, holds a THREE.Group in the scene, and permanently occupies one of 8 MOBMAX slots — degrading the now-verified spawn ecology. One line in updMobs: if(!isFinite(M.y)||M.y<-32){rmMob(i,false);continue;} plus arg validation in spawnMob. Harness-only exposure today, but it is the cheapest verified-fail flip on the board.

top_gaps.title: Chunk-data eviction using the proven editB diff
top_gaps.detail: chunks Map grows ~16KB per visited chunk forever, carried a 4th round; CCmap additionally cliff-clears 4e5 structs causing an fbm recompute storm across every loaded chunk. The blocker is already solved: editB provably reconstructs player edits per chunk (save/load round-trip verified again this round, torches included). Evict chunk data beyond RD+4 alongside dropMesh; regenerate on return. Medium risk because eviction bugs corrupt terrain — gate behind a determinism re-probe.

top_gaps.title: Split frame guard + surface loop errors into __vox.errors
top_gaps.detail: loop()'s single warn-once try/catch (index.html:1080) now shields physics, particles, mining, drops, mobs, farming, chunks, sky, AND render — a persistent throw in any one silently freezes everything after one console line, and the zero-error evidence chain goes blind to it because loop errors never reach __vox.errors. Split sim vs render guards, count errors, push into __vox.errors. Pure insurance for the benchmark's most valuable asset: the verified zero-error streak.

top_gaps.title: CCmap ring eviction instead of clear()
top_gaps.detail: CI() nukes all 400k cached column structs at the threshold (index.html:269); the cache is now hit by the per-second spawner, lightAtW, buildChunk's 18x18 precompute, HUD, and findStructures. Replace clear() with distance-keyed eviction (or two-generation rotation) so the working set near the player survives. Small, contained, removes both the tens-of-MB ceiling and the recompute spike.

top_gaps.title: Pooled remesh scratch buffers before remesh frequency multiplies again
top_gaps.detail: buildChunk still allocates 9 fresh Float32BufferAttributes plus untyped JS arrays per build; torch place/break now triggers up to 4 builds per click and farming growth adds 1Hz remesh events. Verified non-biting at 120 FPS, but smelting/weather/light lanes will multiply rebuild frequency further. Reuse module-level typed-array scratch with high-water sizing. Medium risk: geometry-buffer reuse bugs produce visible mesh corruption — needs a screenshot regression pass.

top_gaps.title: Run mining/placing timers on the sim clock to close the step() seam
top_gaps.detail: mineTick's mtLast and nextPlace use performance.now() while __vox.step() feeds synthetic timestamps up to 30s ahead, so post-step real frames floor mdt to 0 — the probe's stone-mine wart, and the reason hold-to-break rhythm and tool mining speeds keep landing in the unverified pile. Drive both from accumulated sim dt (or clamp mtLast on step exit). Cheap, and it converts already-coded tool-speed behavior into bankable evidence next probe.

top_gaps.title: Autosave debounce + quota-failure surfacing
top_gaps.detail: save() stringifies the whole edits Map every 15s on the main thread and swallows setItem throws (index.html:506-509). Debounce on edit-count delta, and show one HUD warning when the quota throws so long sessions don't silently stop persisting. Carried from round 3 unchanged.

top_gaps.title: Per-type AI function table before mob #3
top_gaps.detail: updMobs inlines zombie/pig behavior as if-else inside the shared hot loop; sun-burn, flee, and attack timers are already type-conditional. Extract MOBDEF[type].ai(M,dt) writing wScx/wScz — a pure refactor that keeps the next creature (skeleton/sheep/creeper lane is inevitable) from multiplying branch surface inside the per-frame path and risking the zero-error streak.

panel.summary: Through the perf/stability lens this is the payoff round: last panel's top-priced gap (a deterministic sim-advance hook) landed exactly as specified, fixed the probe-starvation that was the binding constraint on evidence conversion, and the result is the best-instrumented session of the run — ~40s of deterministic sim, zero errors across two loads, and a measured 0.013 ms/frame sim cost with 8 zombies, proving the entity layer is effectively free. Two new systems (farming, cave light + torches) landed with genuine engineering discipline — bounded player-action-keyed registries, an allocation-free per-build light precompute, remesh scope capped at 4 chunks — and the knockback fail that held last round's score down is fixed and verified, so I move from 38 to 40. The debit side is unchanged in kind: the five carried foundations (unbounded chunks Map, CCmap cliff-clear, warn-once frame guard, unpooled remesh buffers, autosave stringify) are now four rounds old while the feature surface above them keeps growing, and the round added one new verified fail — vertically-escaped mobs leak forever because despawn is horizontal-only, a one-line guard. Net delta versus round 4's panel (37.5 merged, perf 38): both of my round-4 holds (knockback, unverifiable spawn/light-gating clauses) were released by verification rather than by luck, which is precisely how this build keeps earning its stability score; next round's cheapest perf wins are the containment guard, the sim-clock seam fix (which banks tool-speed evidence), and finally spending one lane on the chunk-eviction debt before a third entity type arrives.

lanes.name: Lane 1 — Containment fail + clock seam + torch pop + guard split (robustness)
lanes.goal: Clear the round's only verified fail (vertically-escaped mobs leak forever) and the three judge-filed warts: the mineHold synthetic-clock seam that keeps poisoning stepped-sim mining evidence, floating torches when support breaks, and the warn-once frame guard that hides loop errors from __vox.errors. Zero new feature surface; pure hardening priced ~0.65 MPS combined by the panel.
lanes.spec: All edits in game/index.html. (1) MOB CONTAINMENT — in spawnMob (~line 741), after the `if(!D||mobs.length>=MOBMAX)return false;` guard add `if(!isFinite(x)||!isFinite(z))return false;` and, after the `if(y===undefined||y===null)y=CI(...).h+1;` default (~742), add `if(!isFinite(y))return false;`. In updMobs (~line 806) replace `if(pd>56){rmMob(i,false);continue;}` with `if(!isFinite(pd)||pd>56||!isFinite(M.y)||M.y<-16){rmMob(i,false);continue;}` — the isFinite checks are load-bearing because NaN pd never exceeds 56. (2) SIM CLOCK — introduce a single monotonic sim clock so mining/placing/eating timers work identically under real rAF frames and __vox.step()'s synthetic frames. Declare `let simNow=performance.now();` immediately above `let mL=false,mR=false,nextPlace=0;` (~line 587) and change `mtLast=performance.now()` (~line 664) to `mtLast=simNow`. In simStep (~line 1064) drop the `now` parameter and advance the clock: `function simStep(dt){simNow+=dt*1000;gt+=dt;physics(dt);updParts(dt);mineTick(simNow);updDrops(dt);updMobs(dt);updFarm(dt);}`. Update both call sites: loop's `simStep(dt,now)` -> `simStep(dt)` (~1071) and step()'s `simStep(fx,t0+t*1000)` -> `simStep(fx)` (~1135; t0 no longer needed). Change the mousedown right-click `nextPlace=performance.now()+260` (~591) to `nextPlace=simNow+260`, and in eatFood (~1044) change `const n=performance.now()` to `const n=simNow` (eatT stays in the same units). Leave lastW double-tap (real input path) on performance.now(). (3) TORCH SUPPORT-POP — in doBreak (~638-639) extend the cascade: `let yy=h.y+1,ab=getB(h.x,yy,h.z),popT=false; while(ab===12||ab===13||ab===14||(ab>=19&&ab<=21)||ab===22){ if(ab===22){spawnDrop(h.x,yy,h.z,22);popT=true;} setB(h.x,yy,h.z,0);yy++;ab=getB(h.x,yy,h.z);}` (setB already removes id-22 cells from torchR when old===22, ~line 338). Change the final remesh line (~640) to `if(h.b===22||popT)remeshTorch(h.x,h.z);else touched(h.x,h.y,h.z);` so the 7-block pool updates. (4) GUARD SPLIT — in loop() (~1065-1080) replace the single try/catch with two: guard A around the sim section (dt/fps bookkeeping, simStep-or-camera-hold, sampReq drain), guard B around the world/render section (updChunks, updSky, updHL, updHUD, renderer.render). Each catch warns once via its own flag (`simErr`, `rndErr`) but ALWAYS pushes `{msg:'sim: '+String(e&&e.message||e)}` / `{msg:'render: '+...}` into window.__vox.errors when TEST && window.__vox && length<300. Never rethrow; rAF stays alive. DO NOT touch: physics(), coll(), worldgen, lightOf/lightAtW, spawnTick gating, save format. NOTE for serial integration: lanes 2 and 3 will each append one call inside simStep and lane 2 adds a branch in doBreak — keep your edits surgical so theirs apply cleanly.
lanes.acceptance: On ?test=1 via __vox: (a) `spawnMob('zombie')` called with bad arity / non-finite coords returns false and mobs() stays empty; `spawnMob('zombie', px+2, -20, pz)` followed by `step(2)` leaves mobs() empty (culled at y<-16); after `setSpawning(true)`+`setTime(0)`+`tickSpawns(6)`+`step(5)`, no mob in mobs() reports y<-16 or non-finite coords. (b) setCam aiming at a surface stone block, bare hand: `mineHold(true)` then `step(3)` -> `block(x,y,z)===0` and dropList() contains the drop; same flow with a wood pick completes within step(1) — stepped-sim mining now completes deterministically. (c) place a torch on a grass block, then brk() the support block: torches() no longer lists it, dropList() contains id 22, lightAt at the old head position loses its torch component, and a screenshot shows no floating torch. (d) window.__vox.errors exists, starts empty, and stays empty across ~40s of step() sim plus two loads (and code-read confirms both guards push tagged messages on catch). (e) Regression: fresh ?test=1 spawns at exactly (116.5, 29.02, 115.5) seed 1337; findStructures(300) is bit-identical to round 5; eat()/+hunger math unchanged; zero console errors.

lanes.name: Lane 2 — Smelting family: furnace block, iron ingot, cooked porkchop
lanes.goal: Flip the weight-2 smelting family from confirmed-absent (the file's only mention is the line-230 comment 'smelting doesn't exist yet') and retire the acknowledged iron-ORE-pick substitution. Three judges priced this 0.7-1.0 as the cheapest full-family flip; it reuses the five-round-verified inventory/crafting islot substrate.
lanes.spec: All edits in game/index.html. (1) TILES — atlas tiles 0-39 are used; add T(40) furnace front (stone-noise base like tile 3 with a dark 6x4 opening at bottom-center plus 2-3 ember pixels 255,120,30), T(41) furnace side (plain stone-noise, slightly darker than tile 3), T(42) iron ingot icon (stacked light-grey 214,214,224 bar trapezoids on transparent), T(43) cooked porkchop icon (copy the tile-31 shape but browned: ~165,96,52 outer / 200,140,90 inner). (2) TABLES — BL[23]={t:[41,41,40]}; NAMES[23]='Furnace', NAMES[104]='Iron Ingot', NAMES[110]='Cooked Porkchop'; DROPS[23]=23; HARD[23]=2.2; TOOLREQ[23]=1; ITILE[104]=42, ITILE[110]=43; FOOD[110]=8 and change FOOD[106] from 8 to 4 (MC-direction parity: raw porkchop weak, cooked strong — flag the change in the header comment block). RECIPES: add {in:{3:5},out:23,n:1} (5 stone in the pocket grid — pragmatic substitution for MC's 8-in-3x3, comment it) and change the iron pick recipe from {in:{11:3,100:2},out:103,n:1} to {in:{104:3,100:2},out:103,n:1}; update the line-229/230 comment to say smelting now exists and ingots are required. (3) FURNACE STATE — module-level `const furnaces=new Map();` keyed 'x,y,z' -> {inp:stack|null,fuel:stack|null,out:stack|null,burn:0,cook:0}. `const SMELT={11:104,106:110};` `const FUEL={10:40,6:7.5,16:7.5};` (seconds of burn; coal ore=8 items at 5s each). `function updFurnace(dt)` appended to simStep (one added call after updFarm(dt) — coordinate with lane 1's simStep refactor, which lands first): for each entry, smeltable = inp && SMELT[inp.id] && (out===null || (out.id===SMELT[inp.id] && out.count<64)); if burn<=0 && smeltable && fuel: consume 1 fuel (null at 0), burn=FUEL[fuelId]; if burn>0: burn-=dt; if smeltable: cook+=dt, when cook>=5 {cook=0; inp.count--, null at 0; out merge/create 1x SMELT id} else cook=0; if burn<=0 && !fuel, cook decays to 0. Map is bounded by player placement. (4) PLACE/OPEN — in tryPlace (~line 646), next to the table check add `if(h&&h.b===23&&!sneak){openFurnace(h.x,h.y,h.z);mR=false;return;}`. Placing item id 23 works through the existing generic path (id<100 placeable); on placement `furnaces.set(key,{inp:null,fuel:null,out:null,burn:0,cook:0})` if absent (do this in setB? No — keep setB pure; lazily create the entry in openFurnace/furnacePut instead, via a `furnState(x,y,z)` get-or-create helper that first verifies getB(x,y,z)===23). (5) UI — extend the inventory panel: add a mode flag `furnKey=null`; `openFurnace(x,y,z)` mirrors openInv() (uiOpen=true, key reset, exit pointer lock) but builds a FURNACE panel: h3 'FURNACE', a row of three islots [input, fuel(below-style ok in same row), output] + '→' arrow + a burn/progress text element ('burn 12s · 60%') refreshed from renderInv; input and fuel slots use genClick against a 2-array view, output slot click takes-only (mirror outClick semantics without recipe matching); E / backdrop click closes via the existing closeInv path (extend closeInv: if furnKey, write slots back into the furnaces entry instead of spilling, then furnKey=null — contents STAY in the furnace, they are not returned to inventory). While the furnace UI is open, renderInv refreshes each updCounts call so step()-driven smelting is visible live. (6) BREAK — in doBreak add `if(h.b===23){const f=furnaces.get(h.x+','+h.y+','+h.z);if(f){for(const s of[f.inp,f.fuel,f.out])if(s)for(let i=0;i<s.count;i++)spawnDrop(h.x,h.y,h.z,s.id);furnaces.delete(h.x+','+h.y+','+h.z);}}` before the existing drop logic (distinct branch from lane 1's cascade edit). (7) SAVE — save() (~506) gains `furn:[...furnaces.entries()]` in the JSON; the boot loader (~498) restores `if(Array.isArray(SV.furn))for(const[k,v]of SV.furn)furnaces.set(k,v);` — keep SV.v===1, the field is optional so old saves load fine. (8) __vox hooks — `furnaceInfo:(x,y,z)=>{const f=furnaces.get(x+','+y+','+z);return f?{inp:f.inp,fuel:f.fuel,out:f.out,burn:+f.burn.toFixed(2),cook:+f.cook.toFixed(2)}:null;}`, `furnacePut:(x,y,z,which,id,n)=>` (get-or-create via furnState, set {id,count:n||1} into 'inp'|'fuel'|'out', return true), `openFurnace:(x,y,z)=>openFurnace(x,y,z)`. DO NOT touch: worldgen/genChunk (the furnace is never naturally generated), lightOf, mobs, physics, the 2x2/3x3 crafting flow.
lanes.acceptance: On ?test=1 via __vox: (a) give(3,5) then craft(23)===true; select it, place() on open ground, block(cx,cy,cz)===23; hotbar screenshot shows a furnace thumbnail with NAME label, plus textured (non-blank) icons for ids 104/110 after give(104,1)+give(110,1). (b) furnacePut(cx,cy,cz,'inp',11,3) + furnacePut(cx,cy,cz,'fuel',10,1), then step(20) -> furnaceInfo(...).out equals {id:104,count:3} and fuel is consumed; mid-run (after step(7)) burn>0 and cook is advancing. (c) With only raw ore: clear inventory of 104, give(11,3)+give(100,2) -> craft(103)===false; give(104,3) -> craft(103)===true — ingot-gated iron pick verified. (h) Regression: fresh ?test=1 spawn exactly (116.5, 29.02, 115.5); findStructures(300) identical; torch and bread recipes still craft with exact consumption; zero console errors; no frame hitch while a furnace burns (HUD FPS steady).

lanes.name: Lane 3 — Weather family: biome-coupled rain/snow with sky dimming and rain audio
lanes.goal: Flip the weight-2 weather family from confirmed-absent (grep-clean today): deterministic gt-seeded rain episodes, rain over plains/forest, snowfall in snowy biome and above the verified h>=45 line, nothing in desert, storm sky-dimming through updSky, terrain-stopped particles, a rain audio bed, and probe hooks so evidence lands the same round it ships. Three judges priced this 0.7-0.9.
lanes.spec: All edits in game/index.html. (1) SCHEDULE — pure function of gt, no saved state (gt already persists, so weather is save/load-coherent and bit-deterministic across loads): `function weatherAt(t){const ep=Math.floor(t/90),r=h2f(ep,9341);if(r>=.4)return 0;const f=(t-ep*90)/90;return sst(.04,.18,f)*sst(.97,.82,f);}` Episode = 90s window, ~40% rainy, smooth ramp in/out. Module state: `let wxOverride=null,wI=0,wKind='none';`. (2) UPDATE — `function updWeather(dt)` called from simStep after updFarm (one appended call; lane 1's simStep refactor lands first, lane 2 appends updFurnace before yours — add updWeather after it): compute `wI=wxOverride!==null?wxOverride:weatherAt(gt);` and `const cb=CI(Math.floor(pos.x),Math.floor(pos.z)); wKind=(wI<=0)?'none':(cb.biome==='snowy'||cb.h>=45)?'snow':(cb.biome==='desert')?'none':'rain';` then advance particles. (3) PARTICLES — dedicated buffer, NOT the 320-cap burst system: `const WXN=600` with Float32Array(WXN*3) position attribute on its own THREE.Points (frustumCulled=false, PointsMaterial size .11, transparent, depthWrite false), plus a parallel JS array of {x,y,z} world-space cells initialized lazily. Active count = Math.floor(wI*WXN) when wKind!=='none', exposed via setDrawRange. Per active particle per updWeather tick: rain falls 18/s straight down; snow falls 2.2/s with x+=Math.sin(gt*1.7+i)*.6*dt drift. Kill-and-respawn when y < CI(Math.floor(x),Math.floor(z)).h+1 (terrain stop — never visible inside caves/under roofs... of terrain) or y < pos.y-20; respawn at pos.x+(rand-.5)*44, pos.y+10+rand*16, pos.z+(rand-.5)*44 using Math.random() (visual-only, no determinism contract on particle positions). Set material color per kind each tick: rain (.62,.68,.78) multiplied by matO.color, snow (1,1,1). Because updWeather lives in simStep, pause() freezes precipitation exactly like physics/particles. (4) SKY DIMMING — in updSky (~900-921), after gDL is set: `const wd=(wKind==='none')?0:wI;` blend `skyC.lerp(tmpC.setRGB(.42,.46,.52),.45*wd)` BEFORE the underwater branch and fog copy, multiply the world-light L by (1-.25*wd) when computing lr/lg2/lb, and add `clouds.material.opacity=Math.min(.85,.14+.36*dl+.35*wd)` replacing the existing opacity line. Guard: updSky also runs when paused/before start, and wI/wKind are module state updated by simStep, so reads are always safe (init 0/'none'). (5) AUDIO — lazy rain bed: in audioInit, after the wind source, create `rainSrc` (nbuf loop) -> lowpass 900Hz -> `rainG=AC.createGain()` (gain 0) -> master, start it; in updWeather lerp `rainG.gain.value+=((wKind==='rain'?.07*wI:0)-rainG.gain.value)*Math.min(1,dt*3)` (guard rainG existence). logS('rain') once when wI crosses above .05 (track prev). Add a one-shot test gen `function sRainBurst(){if(!AC)return;const t=AC.currentTime,s=AC.createBufferSource();s.buffer=nbuf;s.loop=true;const f=AC.createBiquadFilter();f.type='lowpass';f.frequency.value=900;const g=AC.createGain();g.gain.setValueAtTime(.12,t);g.gain.exponentialRampToValueAtTime(.001,t+.4);s.connect(f);f.connect(g);g.connect(master);s.start();s.stop(t+.4);}` and append ['rain',sRainBurst] to the audioCheck gens list (~1140). (6) HUD — in updHUD append `+(wI>.1&&wKind!=='none'?'  |  '+wKind:'')`. (7) __vox — `weather:()=>({kind:wKind,intensity:+wI.toFixed(2),scheduled:+weatherAt(gt).toFixed(2)})` and `setWeather:(v)=>{wxOverride=(v===null||v===undefined)?null:Math.max(0,Math.min(1,+v));return wxOverride;}`. DO NOT touch: worldgen, genChunk, CI fields, spawn gating, save format, the burst-particle system, the mesher, lightOf.
lanes.acceptance: On ?test=1 via __vox: (a) at plains/forest, setTime(12), setWeather(1) -> weather() returns {kind:'rain',intensity:1}; screenshot shows falling streaks; skyInfo().hex is measurably darker/greyer than the same setTime(12) with setWeather(0) (compare hex values). (b) setCam to the verified snowy summit (~148, 52, 578): setWeather(1) -> kind 'snow', screenshot shows white slow-drifting flakes; teleport to a desert column (findable via colInfo scan) -> kind 'none' and a screenshot shows zero precipitation. (c) Terrain stop: inside the (64,18,158) cavern with setWeather(1), screenshots at two angles show no particles indoors. (d) pause() then two screenshots ~1s apart are pixel-identical in the precipitation region (frozen), and weather().intensity is unchanged; resume() restarts motion. (e) audioCheck() now returns a 'rain' key with peak > 0.01; with setWeather(1) over plains, __vox.sfx contains a {t:'rain'} entry after the episode-start crossing. (f) Determinism: setWeather(null), then on two fresh ?test=1 loads setTime to the same value -> weather().scheduled identical to 2 decimals on both; spawn exactly (116.5, 29.02, 115.5); findStructures(300) bit-identical. (g) Perf: with setWeather(1) active, step(10) completes with __vox.errors empty and HUD FPS steady when rendering; no new console errors across two loads.

built.lane: Lane 1 — Containment fail + clock seam + torch pop + guard split (robustness)
built.changes_summary: All edits in game/index.html. (1) MOB CONTAINMENT: spawnMob (~line 743) now rejects non-finite x/z before the y-default and non-finite y after it (bad arity returns false); updMobs cull (~line 809) is now `if(!isFinite(pd)||pd>56||!isFinite(M.y)||M.y<-16){rmMob(i,false);continue;}` so NaN-distance and vertically-escaped mobs are removed. (2) SIM CLOCK: new `let simNow=performance.now()` above the mL/mR decls (~587); simStep is now `simStep(dt)` and advances `simNow+=dt*1000` before calling mineTick(simNow) (~1067); loop calls simStep(dt) (~1075); __vox.step() calls simStep(fx) with t0 removed (~1142); mousedown right-click nextPlace (~592), mtLast init (~665), and eatFood cooldown (~1047) all moved from performance.now() to simNow. lastW double-tap and updChunks budget intentionally stay on real time. (3) TORCH SUPPORT-POP: doBreak cascade (~638-640) now includes ab===22, spawns a torch drop (id 22), sets popT, and ends with `if(h.b===22||popT)remeshTorch(...)` so the 7-block light pool remeshes; setB's existing old===22 path cleans torchR. (4) GUARD SPLIT: loop() (~1068-1090) split into guard A (dt/fps bookkeeping, simStep-or-camera-hold, sampReq drain) and guard B (updChunks/updSky/updHL/updHUD/render); each warns once via simErr/rndErr flags but always pushes {msg:'sim: ...'} / {msg:'render: ...'} into window.__vox.errors when TEST && __vox && length<300; never rethrows, rAF stays alive. Verified live against the running preview on ?test=1: (a) spawnMob('zombie') / NaN / Infinity all return false with mobs() empty; y=-20 spawn culled within step(2); night tickSpawns(6)+step(5) yields 4 mobs, zero with y<-16 or non-finite coords. (b) placed surface stone: bare hand breaks within step(3) (2.2s), wood pick within step(1) (0.704s) with drop id 3 in dropList — stepped-sim mining is now deterministic. (c) torch on grass support, brk() support: block cleared, torches() empty, dropList gains id 22, lightAt torch component 1→0, screenshot shows no floating torch (only the item drop). (d) __vox.errors starts empty and stays empty across 40s of step() and two fresh loads; zero console errors (only the pre-existing three.js CDN deprecation warning). (e) fresh ?test=1 spawn exactly (116.5, 29.02, 115.5) seed 1337; findStructures(300) returns the deterministic 5-structure list (3 wells, 2 ruins — worldgen untouched); eat math intact (10→14→18 with the 400ms cooldown now honored under stepped sim).
built.notes: Three notes. (1) Acceptance (b) as written expects a drop from BARE-HAND stone mining, but TOOLREQ[3]=1 tier-gates stone drops (pre-existing, documented design: "ore drops gated by pickaxe tier"); bare hand breaks the block with no drop, wood pick drops id 3. I treated the tier gate as authoritative — flag to the judge if they meant otherwise. (2) For lanes 2/3 serial integration: simStep signature is now simStep(dt) and it advances simNow internally — appended calls slot in after updFarm(dt) cleanly; doBreak's cascade introduced popT but the structure is unchanged otherwise, so lane 2's branch should apply. (3) Harness ergonomics (pre-existing, not changed): the player free-falls during step() if setCam leaves them airborne, so stepped-sim aiming tests should toggle fly first — my verification scripts did. /tmp/vox_main.js is a scratch extraction artifact, safe to delete.

Logic simulated in isolation (node replica of updFurnace + matchRecipe): (b) 3 ore + 1 coal -> after step(7) burn=32.98/cook=2.00 advancing, after step(20) out={id:104,count:3} fuel=null; (d) porkchop cooks in 10s, hunger 10->18 cooked / 10->14 raw exact; recipe table: 5 stone->23, 3 stone->nothing, ore+sticks no longer matches 103, ingots+sticks->103 (also gates __vox.craft via invCount). Risks/follow-ups: (1) lane 1's simStep refactor had not landed in the file I read — if it lands after me it must preserve the updFurnace(dt) call; (2) declaration-order: furnaces/SMELT/FUEL sit before the boot loader (required for SV.furn restore) while furnKey/renderFurn are later let/function bindings — safe because updFurnace only runs from simStep after full script evaluation; (3) furnace burn continues (wasted) after input empties, matching MC behavior; cook resets instantly rather than decaying gradually when fuel dies — spec said 'decays to 0', instant reset chosen for simplicity; (4) per-frame cost is one early-out size check when no furnaces exist, and renderFurn (3 small canvas redraws) only while the panel is open — frame budget unaffected; (5) browser-level acceptance (screenshots, ?test=1 spawn regression, save/reload round-trip) not run here — static + simulated verification only.

built.lane: Lane 3 — Weather family: biome-coupled rain/snow with sky dimming and rain audio
built.notes: Static checks pass: file ends with </html>; extracted main script passes `node --check`; weatherAt verified in node (deterministic, range [0,1], 86/200 episodes rainy under seed 1337). Two deliberate micro-additions beyond the verbatim spec snippets, both flagged: (a) setWeather calls updWeather(0) so wI/wKind/drawRange reflect the override inside a single synchronous eval (dt=0 -> no particle motion, no gain change, no gt advance, determinism untouched) — without it weather() right after setWeather would be one frame stale; (b) wxSpawn clamps respawn y to terrain+2, preventing a one-frame particle flicker inside caves when the player is underground (supports acceptance c). Risks: snow renders pure white (per spec) so it does not take night dimming — may look bright in night screenshots; underwater skyC overwrite drops the storm tint underwater (spec-ordered, intentional); when weather stops and the player travels far before it resumes, stale snow cells can take a few seconds to fall to terrain and respawn near the player (rain self-heals in <1s at 18/s). Rain bed only exists after audioInit (user gesture / lock); in pure headless ?test=1 without a click, rainG stays null and the lerp is guarded — audioCheck evidence comes from sRainBurst instead. Per-tick cost is 600 cached-CI lookups + one sin per snow particle; no per-frame allocations after lazy init.
